column6: Age-specific model ranking in ForecastR App comparing various naive models; time series models; and sibling regressions.  Age 3 used time series with exponential smoothing and Box-Cox transformation.  Age 4 used simple sib reg. Age 5 used sib reg with time-varying slope parameter (Kalman Filter). Age 6 used simple sib reg.
column9: NONE TESTED. ForecastR currently has covariate option only for sibling regressions. Analyses of environmental covariates have typically focused on freshwater factors or early ocean conditions. Based on this consideration; we explored environmental covariates only for those stocks where the older age and younger age in the sibling regression enter the ocean in different years. This focused env covar testing on 2 of the 14 stocks; both in Bristol Bay (Kvichak and Naknek).

Age 3 used time series model with exponential smoothing and BoxCox transform.  Age 4 used log-power sib reg.  Age 5 used time series ARIMA model with Box-Cox transform. Age 6 used simple sib reg.
column9: NONE IN THE TOP RANKED MODELS.  ForecastR currently has covariate option only for sibling regressions. Analyses of environmental covariates have typically focused on freshwater factors or early ocean conditions. Based on this consideration; we explored environmental covariates only for those stocks where the older age and younger age in the sibling regression enter the ocean in different years. KVICHAK is one of 2 stocks where that applies. 5 env covar selected from the PSEC data set and the Salmon Prize Env Covar Handout were matched based on ocean entry year; then tested individually and in combination (incl interaction terms): *CTC_PDO_SummerMean*: Mean PDO anomaly over the summer; using the values from the Chinook Technical Committee of the Pacific Salmon Commission; *CTC_NPGO_SummerMean*: Mean NPGO anomaly over the summer; using the values used by the Chinook Technical Committee of the Pacific Salmon Commission; *MEIv2MeanJunToAug*: Mean MEI from Jun to Aug; using the values from the NOAA Ocean Conditions Index data set; *ONIAvgJanToJun*: Mean ONI from Jan to Jun; using the values from the NOAA Ocean Conditions Index data set; *Pacea_NPI_Anomaly*: North Pacific Index annual values from the PACEA package.
